building.body: A global standard for sector-specific calculation intelligence, expanding across industries while keeping every formula governed, auditable, and trustworthy.
building.title: What we are building

different.body: Every result carries a transparent calculation summary, indicative regional benchmarks, and documented assumptions you can review with your team before acting.
different.title: What makes us different

hero.lead: SectorCalc is a global industrial calculation and decision support platform that helps businesses expose hidden losses and act on them with confidence.
hero.title: Computation, turned into reliable decisions.

how.body: You provide the inputs you know; the system resolves what is required, runs governed deterministic calculations, and validates every result against invariants and benchmarks before showing it.
how.title: How our system works

manifesto.primary: A platform to expose hidden losses,
manifesto.secondary: and transform computation into reliable decision support.

problem.body: Teams make cost, quote, and risk decisions on incomplete information. SectorCalc makes the invisible measurable, so decisions rest on evidence instead of guesswork.
problem.title: The problem we solve

aboutPage.seoDescription: SectorCalc helps businesses expose hidden losses, quantify operational risks, and turn industrial calculations into reliable decision support.
aboutPage.seoTitle: About SectorCalc | Industrial Calculation & Decision Support

what.body: A focused suite of sector calculators and decision reports that turn raw operational inputs into clear, defensible numbers, not another spreadsheet or a heavyweight ERP.
what.title: What SectorCalc is

who.body: Owner-operators, estimators, field teams, and consultants who need fast, reliable answers without hiring a full pricing desk or deploying enterprise software.
who.title: Who it is built for

why.body: Most operational losses never appear on an invoice. They hide in pricing, scrap, setup time, efficiency drops, and weak quoting discipline, and they erode margins quietly for years.
why.title: Why SectorCalc exists

assistant.disclaimer: Guidance only. SectorCalc does not provide financial, legal, or engineering advice, and the assistant never runs calculations.
assistant.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

assistant.placeholder: Ask about tools, inputs, or results…

reply.about: SectorCalc is a global industrial calculation and decision-support platform that exposes hidden losses and turns computation into reliable decisions:
reply.approvedReports: Premium results can be saved as exportable decision summaries your team can review internally.
reply.benchmarks: SectorCalc shows indicative regional benchmark context next to your result — for orientation, not official figures.
reply.blockedFormula: I can't run calculations or share formulas. Open the relevant tool and it will compute the result for you.
reply.blockedPrivate: I can't access private data, accounts, or secrets, and I can't bypass any gate.
reply.enterprise: For team usage, reporting governance, bundles, and procurement, see the enterprise page:
reply.explainTool: This tool turns your job inputs into a clear verdict with the risk drivers behind it:
reply.fallback: I can help you find the right tool, understand inputs and results, and navigate SectorCalc. Try one of these:
reply.findTool: Here are tools that match what you described:
reply.inputs: You enter the core costs, quantities, and rates for your job. Required fields are kept minimal, and optional fields sharpen the result.
reply.pricing: You can see the plans and what each includes on the pricing page:
reply.regionalUnits: SectorCalc supports metric, imperial, and regional units, and shows the conversions it used.
reply.results: The result is a verdict with the metrics and risk drivers behind it, so you can see why it lands where it does and what to adjust.
reply.trustTrace: Calculation Summary records inputs, assumptions, and validation steps so you can see how the result was derived.

freeTool.estimateMissesBody: This free result gives the first number. It does not include hidden drivers such as thresholds, benchmark comparison, sensitivity, export-ready report output or suggested action plans.
freeTool.faqFreeAnswer: Yes. SectorCalc free calculators run in your browser with no sign-up required.
freeTool.faqPremiumAnswer: Use a premium calculator when the estimate affects pricing, operations or management decisions and you need hidden drivers, thresholds and export-ready reports.
freeTool.faqPremiumTitle: When should I use a premium calculator?
freeTool.faqUseAnswer: %1$s gives a quick formula-based estimate from the values you enter. Use it for sanity checks before quoting, ordering or planning.
freeTool.faqUseTitle: What is this calculator used for?
freeTool.includes1: Formula-based estimate from the inputs you enter
freeTool.includes2: Primary result plus supporting secondary values where applicable
freeTool.includes3: Plain-language explanation of what the numbers represent

premium.decidesBody: Use this calculator when you need to decide whether current inputs sit inside acceptable exposure bands, which driver to investigate first, and whether to reprice, reschedule or redesign before repeating the work.
premium.faqErpAnswer: No. SectorCalc is a calculator and decision-report layer. It does not replace ERP, accounting systems or professional financial, legal or engineering advice.
premium.faqErpTitle: Is this a replacement for ERP or professional advice?
premium.faqIsFreeAnswer: This tool is part of the premium analysis scope. Full results and decision summaries require credits or an active Pro subscription.
premium.faqMeasureAnswer: %1$s measures hidden loss exposure and operating drivers from the inputs you provide, with threshold interpretation against stated bands.
premium.faqPremiumAnswer: Use a premium calculator when the estimate affects pricing, operations or management decisions and you need hidden drivers, thresholds and export-ready reports.
premium.faqPremiumTitle: When should I use a premium calculator?
premium.faqReportAnswer: The decision report includes executive summary, hidden driver breakdown, threshold check, suggested actions, assumption notes and export-ready output on paid access.
premium.faqReportTitle: What is included in the premium decision report?
premium.previewExcludesBody: The free preview shows sample structure only. Full threshold checks, hidden driver breakdown, suggested actions and export-ready PDF or CSV require paid access.
premium.previewExcludesTitle: What the preview does not include
premium.promiseBody: Structured summary with threshold alerts, hidden drivers and export-ready report output.
premium.reportBullet4: Export-ready PDF and CSV on paid access
premium.whenToUseBody: Use this calculator when the result affects pricing, operations, capacity, scheduling or management reporting.

avg_hours_per_comeback.helper: Average technician hours spent diagnosing and re-repairing a comeback (Lean Takt time impact)
avg_hours_per_comeback.label: Average Hours per Comeback

avg_labor_rate.helper: Fully burdened labor rate including overhead (WERC DC Measures)
avg_labor_rate.label: Average Labor Rate

comeback_count.helper: Number of repair orders that returned for rework within warranty or service period (Six Sigma DPMO metric)

customer_lifetime_value.helper: Estimated net profit from a loyal customer over their relationship with the shop
customer_lifetime_value.label: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)

data_quality_score.helper: Subjective score of input data reliability: 1=estimated, 3=measured, 5=audited. Used for confidence adjustment.
data_quality_score.label: Data Quality Score (1-5)

electricity_kwh.helper: Annual electricity usage from utility bills or submeters. Scope 2 emission source.
electricity_kwh.label: Electricity Consumption

fuel_diesel_liters.helper: Annual diesel consumption for owned fleet vehicles. Scope 1 emission source.
fuel_diesel_liters.label: Diesel Fuel Consumption (Fleet)

fuel_gasoline_liters.helper: Annual gasoline consumption for owned fleet vehicles. Scope 1 emission source.
fuel_gasoline_liters.label: Gasoline Fuel Consumption (Fleet)

natural_gas_therms.helper: Annual natural gas usage from utility bills. Scope 1 emission source.
natural_gas_therms.label: Natural Gas Consumption

region.helper: Geographic region determines emission factors for electricity grid and fuel standards.
region.label: Operational Region

waste_tonnes.helper: Total waste sent to landfill or incineration. Scope 3 emission source.
waste_tonnes.label: Waste Generated

energy_consumption.helper: Annual electrical energy used by the facility (ISO 50001).
energy_consumption.label: Total Energy Consumption

energy_source.helper: The dominant source of electricity (grid mix, solar, wind, natural gas).
energy_source.label: Primary Energy Source

fuel_consumption.helper: Total diesel, gasoline, or heating oil used annually (Scope 1 emissions).
fuel_consumption.label: Fossil Fuel Consumption (Fleet & Heating)

has_carbon_offset_program.helper: Indicates if the organization purchases verified carbon credits.
has_carbon_offset_program.label: Active Carbon Offset Program

production_volume.helper: Total units produced per year (for intensity-based benchmarking).
production_volume.label: Annual Production Volume

recycling_rate.helper: Percentage of waste that is recycled or composted (Lean green metrics).
recycling_rate.label: Recycling Rate

transport_distance.helper: Average distance raw materials or finished goods travel (logistics carbon).
transport_distance.label: Average Transport Distance per Unit
transport_distance.placeholder: Enter average transport distance per unit

waste_generated.helper: Annual waste sent to landfill or incineration (WERC guidelines).
waste_generated.label: Total Waste Generated

car_mileage.helper: Total miles driven per month in a gasoline-powered car.
car_mileage.label: Monthly Car Mileage

electricity_kwh.helper: Total kilowatt-hours of electricity consumed per month from utility bills.
electricity_kwh.label: Monthly Electricity Usage

flights_short.helper: Number of short-haul flights (under 500 miles) taken per year.
flights_short.label: Short Flights per Year

gas_therms.helper: Total therms of natural gas used per month for heating or cooking.
gas_therms.label: Monthly Natural Gas Usage

dio.helper: Average number of days inventory is held before sale. Lower DIO reduces holding costs.
dio.label: Days Inventory Outstanding (DIO)

dpo.helper: Average number of days to pay suppliers. Higher DPO conserves cash.
dpo.label: Days Payables Outstanding (DPO)

dso.helper: Average number of days to collect payment after a sale. Lower DSO improves cash flow.
dso.label: Days Sales Outstanding (DSO)

accountspayablechange.helper: Increase in accounts payable (money you owe) adds cash flow; decrease reduces cash. Enter positive for increase, negative for decrease.
accountspayablechange.label: Change in Accounts Payable

accountsreceivablechange.helper: Increase in accounts receivable (money owed to you) reduces cash flow; decrease adds cash. Enter positive for increase, negative for decrease.
accountsreceivablechange.label: Change in Accounts Receivable

depreciation.helper: Non-cash expense that reduces net income but does not affect cash. Added back to calculate cash flow.
depreciation.label: Depreciation

netincome.helper: Total profit after all expenses, taxes, and costs. This is the starting point for cash flow calculation.
netincome.label: Net Income

othernoncashitems.helper: Sum of other non-cash expenses (e.g., amortization, stock-based compensation) that reduce net income but not cash. Add back positive values.
othernoncashitems.label: Other Non-Cash Items

avg_days_payable.helper: Average number of days to pay suppliers. Higher DPO can improve cash flow but may strain supplier relationships.
avg_days_payable.label: Average Days Payable (DPO)

avg_days_receivable.helper: Average number of days to collect payment after a sale. High DSO indicates potential cash flow issues.
avg_days_receivable.label: Average Days Receivable (DSO)

customer_payment_behavior.helper: Historical customer payment behavior. Adjusts effective DSO.
customer_payment_behavior.label: Customer Payment Behavior

inventory_days.helper: Average number of days inventory is held before sale. High DII ties up cash and increases holding costs.
inventory_days.label: Inventory Days (DII)

lean_inventory_flag.helper: Indicates if Lean inventory methods (e.g., JIT) are in place to reduce inventory days.
lean_inventory_flag.label: Lean Inventory Practices Active

operating_expenses_daily.helper: Total daily operating costs including labor, materials, overhead. Used to compute cash gap in monetary terms.
operating_expenses_daily.label: Daily Operating Expenses

payment_terms_suppliers.helper: Standard payment terms offered by suppliers. Affects DPO and cash gap.
payment_terms_suppliers.label: Supplier Payment Terms

revenue_daily.helper: Average daily revenue from sales. Used to compute cash conversion cycle and gap.
revenue_daily.label: Daily Revenue

six_sigma_quality_level.helper: Current process sigma level. Higher sigma indicates fewer defects and less cash tied up in rework/scrap.
six_sigma_quality_level.label: Six Sigma Quality Level (Sigma)

secondary_value.helper: Secondary input for Cash on Cash Return.
secondary_value.label: Secondary value

tertiary_value.helper: Optional modifier for Cash on Cash Return.
tertiary_value.label: Tertiary value

closingcostspercent.helper: Enter the estimated closing costs as a percentage of the loan amount. Typical range is 2-5%. Not financial advice.
closingcostspercent.label: Closing Costs (%)

currenthomevalue.helper: Enter the current market value of your home. This is used to determine the maximum loan amount based on LTV ratio. Not financial advice.
currenthomevalue.label: Current Home Value

desiredcashout.helper: Enter the amount of cash you want to receive after paying off your existing mortgage. Not financial advice.
desiredcashout.label: Desired Cash Out

existingmortgagebalance.helper: Enter the remaining balance on your current mortgage. This will be paid off with the new loan. Not financial advice.
existingmortgagebalance.label: Existing Mortgage Balance

newinterestrate.helper: Enter the annual interest rate for the new loan. Rates vary based on credit and market conditions. Not financial advice.
newinterestrate.label: New Interest Rate (%)

newloantermyears.helper: Enter the term length for the new refinanced loan. Common terms are 15, 20, or 30 years. Not financial advice.
newloantermyears.label: New Loan Term (Years)

cash_and_cash_equivalents.helper: Total cash and cash equivalents available to cover short-term liabilities.
cash_and_cash_equivalents.label: Cash and Cash Equivalents

current_liabilities.helper: Total short-term debts and obligations due within one year.
current_liabilities.label: Current Liabilities

marketable_securities.helper: Short-term, liquid investments that can be quickly converted to cash.
marketable_securities.label: Marketable Securities

carbon_price_origin.placeholder: Enter carbon price paid in country of origin

cbam_certificate_price.helper: Latest weekly average auction price of CBAM certificates.
cbam_certificate_price.label: CBAM Certificate Price (current auction)

compliance_deadline_met.helper: Indicates if the importer submitted the required CBAM report before the quarterly deadline.
compliance_deadline_met.label: Compliance Deadline Met (quarterly report submitted)
compliance_deadline_met.placeholder: Enter compliance deadline met (quarterly report submitted)

embedded_emissions_per_tonne.helper: Direct and indirect GHG emissions embedded in the imported goods (scope 1+2).
embedded_emissions_per_tonne.label: Embedded Emissions per Tonne

free_allocation_factor.helper: Proportion of emissions covered by free allowances in the EU ETS for the product sector.
free_allocation_factor.label: Free Allocation Factor for Sector
free_allocation_factor.placeholder: Enter free allocation factor for sector

total_imported_tonnes.helper: Total mass of CBAM-covered goods imported in the reporting period.
total_imported_tonnes.label: Total Imported Tonnes (steel/aluminum/cement/fertilizer/hydrogen)

verification_status.helper: Whether the embedded emissions data has been independently verified by an accredited body.
verification_status.label: Verification Status of Embedded Emissions
verification_status.placeholder: Enter verification status of embedded emissions

annual_production_volume.helper: Total output of the primary product (e.g., steel, cement, aluminum) in metric tons per year.
annual_production_volume.label: Annual Production Volume

carbon_intensity.helper: Direct and indirect greenhouse gas emissions per ton of product, based on ISO 14064 or GHG Protocol.
carbon_intensity.label: Carbon Intensity

cbam_carbon_price.helper: Expected or current EU ETS allowance price used for CBAM certificate calculation.
cbam_carbon_price.label: CBAM Carbon Price

compliance_readiness_score.helper: Assessment of data collection, reporting systems, and verification readiness per CBAM requirements.
compliance_readiness_score.label: Compliance Readiness Score

emission_scope.helper: Defines which emission scopes are included in the carbon intensity calculation.
emission_scope.label: Emission Scope

free_allocation_percentage.helper: Percentage of emissions covered by free allowances under CBAM transitional rules.
free_allocation_percentage.label: Free Allocation Percentage

industry_benchmark_intensity.helper: Best available technology (BAT) or sector average carbon intensity for benchmarking (ISO 50001).
industry_benchmark_intensity.label: Industry Benchmark Intensity

production_efficiency_index.helper: Overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) or similar lean metric; higher values reduce waste and emissions.
production_efficiency_index.label: Production Efficiency Index

use_verified_data.helper: If true, only data from verified sources (e.g., accredited verifier) is used; otherwise estimated data may be included.
use_verified_data.label: Use Verified Data Only

changeover_time_matrix.helper: Average time to switch from one product to another within the same production line. Based on SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die) standards.
changeover_time_matrix.label: Changeover Time Matrix (minutes)

lean_smed_implemented.helper: Indicates whether Single-Minute Exchange of Die principles have been formally applied.
lean_smed_implemented.label: SMED Implementation Status

desired_margin_percent.helper: Target net profit margin on the bid.
desired_margin_percent.label: Desired Profit Margin (%)

labor_burden_percent.helper: Percentage added to labor rate for taxes, insurance, benefits. Typical range 15-35%.

annual_interest_rate.helper: Enter the annual interest rate as a percentage. This is not financial advice.
annual_interest_rate.label: Annual Interest Rate (%)

closing_cost_rate.helper: Enter the estimated closing costs as a percentage of the purchase price. Typical range is 2-5%. This is not financial advice.
closing_cost_rate.label: Closing Cost Rate (%)

down_payment_percent.helper: Enter the down payment percentage. Typical range is 3-20%. This is not financial advice.
down_payment_percent.label: Down Payment (%)

loan_term_years.helper: Enter the loan term in years. Common terms are 15 or 30 years. This is not financial advice.
loan_term_years.label: Loan Term (Years)

property_tax_rate.helper: Enter the annual property tax rate as a percentage of the purchase price. This is not financial advice.
property_tax_rate.label: Property Tax Rate (%)

purchase_price.helper: Enter the total purchase price of the property. This is not financial advice.
purchase_price.label: Purchase Price

api_call_volume.helper: Total number of API calls made per month, including successful and overrun calls.
api_call_volume.label: Monthly API Call Volume

avg_overrun_duration.helper: Average extra time beyond the SLA threshold for overrun calls.
avg_overrun_duration.label: Average Overrun Duration

cost_per_call.helper: Direct cost per API call (compute, network, storage).
cost_per_call.label: Cost per API Call

data_egress_gb.helper: Total data transferred out of the cloud per month.
data_egress_gb.label: Monthly Data Egress

egress_cost_per_gb.helper: Cost per GB of data egress (varies by provider and region).
egress_cost_per_gb.label: Egress Cost per GB

include_hidden_costs.helper: If true, adds estimated hidden costs like customer churn and brand impact.
include_hidden_costs.label: Include Hidden Costs (e.g., opportunity loss)
include_hidden_costs.placeholder: Enter include hidden costs (e.g., opportunity loss)

monthly_service_fee.helper: Base monthly fee paid to the cloud provider.
monthly_service_fee.label: Monthly Service Fee

overrun_rate.helper: Percentage of API calls that exceed the agreed response time threshold (e.g., >200ms).
overrun_rate.label: Overrun Rate

overrun_threshold_ms.helper: Maximum acceptable response time per the SLA.
overrun_threshold_ms.label: Overrun Threshold (SLA)

region.helper: Geographic region of the cloud service, affects egress costs and latency.
region.label: Cloud Region

retry_cost_per_call.helper: Additional cost incurred when a call is retried due to timeout or error.
retry_cost_per_call.label: Retry Cost per Call

retry_rate.helper: Percentage of overrun calls that trigger a retry.
retry_rate.label: Retry Rate

sla_penalty_percentage.helper: Percentage of monthly service fee credited as penalty if SLA is breached.
sla_penalty_percentage.label: SLA Penalty Percentage

cloud_provider.helper: Primary cloud provider for cost analysis.
cloud_provider.label: Cloud Provider

compute_utilization_rate.helper: Average CPU/memory utilization across all compute instances.
compute_utilization_rate.label: Average Compute Utilization Rate

data_transfer_out_gb.helper: Total outbound data transfer volume per month.
data_transfer_out_gb.label: Monthly Data Transfer Out

idle_resource_count.helper: Number of compute instances that have been idle for more than 7 days.
idle_resource_count.label: Idle Resource Count

include_network_waste.helper: Toggle to include network-related waste (e.g., data transfer, load balancer inefficiencies).
include_network_waste.label: Include Network Waste Analysis

reserved_instance_coverage.helper: Percentage of compute spend covered by reserved or savings plans.
reserved_instance_coverage.label: Reserved Instance Coverage

rightsizing_opportunity_score.helper: Aggregate score (0-100) indicating potential savings from instance type adjustments.
rightsizing_opportunity_score.label: Rightsizing Opportunity Score

storage_waste_percentage.helper: Estimated percentage of storage that is orphaned, unused, or over-provisioned.
storage_waste_percentage.label: Storage Waste Percentage

total_cloud_spend.helper: Total monthly cloud infrastructure cost across all services.
total_cloud_spend.label: Total Cloud Spend (Monthly)

primary_value.label: Primary value

secondary_value.helper: Secondary input for Clustering Calculator.
secondary_value.label: Secondary value

tertiary_value.helper: Optional modifier for Clustering Calculator.
tertiary_value.label: Tertiary value

avg_order_value.helper: Mean revenue per transaction. Used in CLV calculation. Typical range: $20-$200 for B2C, $500-$5000 for B2B.
avg_order_value.label: Average Order Value (AOV)

cac_total.helper: Sum of all marketing and sales costs divided by number of new customers. Includes ad spend, salaries, tools.
cac_total.label: Total Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)

customer_lifetime_years.helper: Average retention period. For subscription models, use 1/churn rate.
customer_lifetime_years.label: Customer Lifetime (Years)

data_confidence.helper: Subjective assessment of input data reliability. Low = estimates, High = audited data.
data_confidence.label: Data Confidence Level

discount_rate.helper: Cost of capital or required rate of return. Typical range: 8-15%.
discount_rate.label: Discount Rate (Annual)

gross_margin_pct.helper: Profit margin after COGS. Typical range: 30-70% for product businesses, 60-90% for SaaS.
gross_margin_pct.label: Gross Margin (%)

include_churn_adjustment.helper: If true, uses retention rate to adjust CLV for churn. Recommended for subscription models.
include_churn_adjustment.label: Include Churn Adjustment

purchase_frequency.helper: Number of purchases per customer per year. Derived from historical data or cohort analysis.
purchase_frequency.label: Purchase Frequency per Year

retention_rate.helper: Percentage of customers retained year over year. Used for churn adjustment. Industry benchmark: 60-90%.
retention_rate.label: Annual Retention Rate

secondary_value.helper: Secondary input for CLV Calculator.
secondary_value.label: Secondary value

tertiary_value.label: Tertiary value

batch_quantity.helper: Number of units in the batch for total cost and waste calculations.
batch_quantity.label: Batch Quantity

conversion_precision.helper: Select the conversion factor precision based on industry standard (ISO 80000-1, NIST).
conversion_precision.label: Conversion Precision

length_cm.helper: Measured length in centimeters, typically from calipers, rulers, or CAD models.
length_cm.label: Length in Centimeters

measurement_uncertainty.helper: Estimated uncertainty of the measurement (e.g., ±0.05 cm for standard calipers).
measurement_uncertainty.label: Measurement Uncertainty

tolerance_class.helper: Tolerance class for dimensional inspection per ISO 2768. Affects alert thresholds.
tolerance_class.label: Tolerance Class

unit_cost_per_inch.helper: Cost per inch of material or product, used for cost estimation in Lean/Six Sigma projects.
unit_cost_per_inch.label: Unit Cost per Inch

primary_value.helper: Primary input for CMYK to RGB.

secondary_value.helper: Secondary input for CMYK to RGB.

tertiary_value.helper: Optional modifier for CMYK to RGB.
tertiary_value.label: Tertiary value

coolant_on_time.helper: Time for coolant to activate and stabilize before cutting.
coolant_on_time.label: Coolant On Time

cutting_length.helper: Total length of the tool path in contact with the workpiece. Derived from CAM or drawing.
cutting_length.label: Cutting Length

feed_rate.helper: Linear feed speed of the cutting tool. Based on material and tool specifications.
feed_rate.label: Feed Rate

machine_efficiency.helper: Overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) factor. Accounts for downtime, speed loss, and quality loss.
machine_efficiency.label: Machine Efficiency

material_hardness_factor.helper: Adjusts cutting time based on material machinability. Refer to ISO 3685.
material_hardness_factor.label: Material Hardness Factor

measurement_time.helper: Time for probing or manual measurement during the cycle.
measurement_time.label: In-Process Measurement Time

number_of_passes.helper: Number of cutting passes required to achieve final geometry.
number_of_passes.label: Number of Passes

number_of_tool_changes.helper: Total tool changes required for the operation.
number_of_tool_changes.label: Number of Tool Changes

part_loading_time.helper: Time to load and unload the workpiece from the machine.
part_loading_time.label: Part Loading/Unloading Time

rapid_traverse_distance.helper: Total distance traveled at rapid traverse (non-cutting moves).
rapid_traverse_distance.label: Rapid Traverse Distance

rapid_traverse_rate.helper: Maximum speed of the machine during rapid moves.
rapid_traverse_rate.label: Rapid Traverse Rate

spindle_speed.helper: Rotational speed of the spindle. Affects cutting speed and surface finish.
spindle_speed.label: Spindle Speed

tool_change_time.helper: Average time to change a tool, including turret indexing.
tool_change_time.label: Tool Change Time

tool_wear_factor.helper: Multiplier for tool wear. 1.0 = new tool, >1.0 = worn tool. Based on tool life data.
tool_wear_factor.label: Tool Wear Factor

batch_size.helper: Number of identical parts produced in one production run.
batch_size.label: Batch Size (parts)

density_g_per_cm3.helper: Material density used to convert volume to weight.
density_g_per_cm3.label: Density (g/cm³)

labor_hourly_rate_usd.helper: Operator wage including overhead and benefits.
labor_hourly_rate_usd.label: Labor Hourly Rate ($/hr)

machine_hourly_rate_usd.helper: Fully burdened machine cost including depreciation, maintenance, and energy.
machine_hourly_rate_usd.label: Machine Hourly Rate ($/hr)

machining_time_min.helper: Total cycle time per part including cutting, tool changes, and rapid moves.
machining_time_min.label: Machining Time (minutes)

material_cost_per_kg_usd.helper: Price of raw material per kilogram, varies by material type.
material_cost_per_kg_usd.label: Material Cost per kg ($/kg)

material_type.helper: Material selection directly impacts tool wear, cutting speed, and cost per part.
material_type.label: Material Type

overhead_percentage.helper: Facility, quality, and administrative overhead as a percentage of direct costs.
overhead_percentage.label: Overhead Percentage (%)

part_weight_kg.helper: Weight of the finished part, used to calculate raw material cost and scrap.

stock_volume_cm3.label: Stock Volume (cm³)

ambient_temperature.helper: Average ambient temperature at compressor intake. Higher temperature reduces air density and increases power.
ambient_temperature.label: Average Ambient Temperature

compressor_power_rating.helper: Nameplate power of the compressor motor. Typically from 5 kW to 5000 kW for industrial units.
compressor_power_rating.label: Compressor Motor Power Rating

compressor_type.helper: Different compressor types have different specific power curves and maintenance profiles.
compressor_type.label: Compressor Type

electricity_cost_per_kwh.helper: Blended average cost per kWh including demand charges. Typical range $0.05-$0.30.
electricity_cost_per_kwh.label: Electricity Cost

has_vsd.helper: VSD can reduce energy consumption by 15-35% under part-load conditions.
has_vsd.label: Variable Speed Drive (VSD) Installed

leakage_percentage.helper: Percentage of compressed air lost through leaks. Typical industrial systems: 20-30%.
leakage_percentage.label: Estimated System Leakage

load_factor.helper: Average percentage of full load capacity at which the compressor operates. Includes part-load and unloaded periods.
load_factor.label: Load Factor (Average % of Full Load)
load_factor.placeholder: Enter load factor (average % of full load)

maintenance_quality.helper: Maintenance quality affects efficiency, leakage, and pressure drop. Poor maintenance can increase energy use by 10-20%.
maintenance_quality.label: Maintenance Quality Level

motor_efficiency.helper: Efficiency of the electric motor driving the compressor. Premium efficiency motors (IE3/IE4) are >90%.
motor_efficiency.label: Motor Efficiency

operating_hours_per_year.helper: Total annual operating hours. Typical industrial: 8000 hours (2 shifts, 5 days).
operating_hours_per_year.label: Operating Hours per Year

pressure_setpoint.helper: Operating pressure at the compressor discharge. Higher pressure increases energy consumption by ~1% per 0.1 bar.
pressure_setpoint.label: System Pressure Setpoint

compressor_efficiency.helper: Overall efficiency of the compressor system (motor + drive + compressor). Typical range 70-85%.
compressor_efficiency.label: Compressor Efficiency

electricity_cost.helper: Average blended cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour.
electricity_cost.label: Electricity Cost

include_maintenance_cost.helper: If true, adds estimated labor and material cost for leak repair.
include_maintenance_cost.label: Include Maintenance Cost

leak_diameter.helper: Diameter of a round orifice representing the average leak size. Common sizes: 1/8", 1/4", 1/2".
leak_diameter.label: Equivalent Leak Diameter

leak_type.helper: Geometry of the leak affects discharge coefficient. Round orifice is most common.
leak_type.label: Leak Type

number_of_leaks.helper: Total number of identified or estimated leaks in the system.
number_of_leaks.label: Number of Leaks

operating_hours.helper: Total hours the compressor runs per year. Continuous operation = 8760 h.
operating_hours.label: Annual Operating Hours

system_pressure.helper: Operating pressure of the compressed air system; higher pressure increases leak flow rate.
system_pressure.label: System Pressure

altitude.helper: Site elevation for air density correction.
altitude.label: Altitude Above Sea Level

ambienttemperature.helper: Average ambient temperature at compressor location.
ambienttemperature.label: Ambient Temperature

applicationtype.helper: Primary use case, influences safety factor.
applicationtype.label: Application Type

compressorcontroltype.helper: Type of compressor capacity control, affects tank sizing factor.
compressorcontroltype.label: Compressor Control Type

compressorflowrate.helper: Rated free air delivery of the compressor at standard conditions.
compressorflowrate.label: Compressor Flow Rate

demandflowrate.helper: Average compressed air consumption of the system.
demandflowrate.label: Average Demand Flow Rate

peakduration.helper: Duration of the peak demand event.
peakduration.label: Peak Demand Duration

pressuredifferential.helper: Maximum acceptable pressure drop during peak event.
pressuredifferential.label: Allowable Pressure Differential

systemleakagepercent.helper: Estimated percentage of compressed air lost to leaks.
systemleakagepercent.label: System Leakage Estimate

systempressure.helper: Nominal pressure at which the system operates.
systempressure.label: System Operating Pressure

cargo_height_cm.helper: Height of the cargo unit. Must respect container door height and internal height.
cargo_height_cm.label: Cargo Height (cm)

cargo_length_cm.helper: Length of the primary cargo unit (pallet or box). Must align with container internal length.
cargo_length_cm.label: Cargo Length (cm)

cargo_weight_kg.helper: Weight per cargo unit. Critical for gross weight limit and axle load distribution.
cargo_weight_kg.label: Cargo Weight per Unit (kg)

cargo_width_cm.helper: Width of the cargo unit. Affects lateral stacking and aisle space.
cargo_width_cm.label: Cargo Width (cm)

container_type.helper: Select the container type to define internal volume and dimensional constraints per ISO 668.
container_type.label: Container Type

load_secure_method.helper: Method used to secure cargo. Affects usable volume and weight distribution per CTU Code.
load_secure_method.label: Load Securing Method

pallet_height_cm.helper: Height of the pallet (if used). Added to cargo height for total unit height.
pallet_height_cm.label: Pallet Height (cm)

stacking_factor.helper: Number of cargo units that can be stacked vertically, considering crush resistance and stability.
stacking_factor.label: Stacking Factor

use_pallet.helper: Indicates if cargo is palletized. Pallet dimensions add to base footprint and reduce usable height.
use_pallet.label: Use Pallet

annual_demand.helper: Total forecasted demand for the SKU over one year. Used to calculate order frequency and cycle stock.
annual_demand.label: Annual Demand (units)

backorder_cost_per_unit.helper: Cost per unit of unmet demand (lost sales, penalty, expediting). Used in total cost trade-off.
backorder_cost_per_unit.label: Backorder Cost per Unit ($)

days_per_year.helper: Number of days the facility operates. Converts annual demand to daily demand.
days_per_year.label: Operating Days per Year

demand_std_dev.helper: Daily demand variability. Critical for safety stock under Six Sigma service levels.
demand_std_dev.label: Demand Standard Deviation (units/day)

holding_cost_per_unit.helper: Cost to hold one unit in inventory for one year (storage, insurance, obsolescence). Key for inventory carrying cost.
holding_cost_per_unit.label: Holding Cost per Unit per Year ($)
holding_cost_per_unit.placeholder: Enter holding cost per unit per year ($)

inventory_policy.helper: Review method: continuous (Q,R) or periodic (P,T). Affects safety stock formula.
inventory_policy.label: Inventory Policy

lead_time_days.helper: Time from order placement to receipt. Used for safety stock calculation and reorder point.
lead_time_days.label: Lead Time (days)

moq.helper: Supplier-imposed minimum batch size. If EOQ < MOQ, order quantity must be adjusted upward, increasing holding cost.
moq.label: Minimum Order Quantity (units)

ordering_cost.helper: Fixed cost per purchase order (setup, administrative, freight). Influences EOQ and total logistics cost.
ordering_cost.label: Ordering Cost per Order ($)

service_level.helper: Desired probability of not stocking out during lead time. Used to derive z-score for safety stock.
service_level.label: Service Level (%)

storage_capacity.helper: Maximum inventory the warehouse can physically hold. Constraint for feasible order quantity.
storage_capacity.label: Storage Capacity (units)

supplier_reliability.helper: Probability that supplier delivers on time. Adjusts safety stock for lead time variability (Lean JIT).
supplier_reliability.label: Supplier Reliability Factor

unit_cost.helper: Purchase cost per unit from supplier. Used in total cost calculations and MOQ financial impact.
unit_cost.label: Unit Cost ($)

use_moq_override.helper: If true, order quantity is rounded up to MOQ when EOQ < MOQ. If false, EOQ is used even if below MOQ (may violate supplier terms).
use_moq_override.label: Force MOQ Compliance

annual_interest_rate.label: Annual Interest Rate

down_payment_percent.helper: Percentage of property price paid upfront. Affects PMI and LTV ratio.
down_payment_percent.label: Down Payment Percentage

property_tax_rate.helper: Annual property tax as percentage of property value. Varies by jurisdiction.

interestrate.label: Interest Rate (%)

loanamount.helper: Enter the total loan amount you plan to borrow. This is the principal before any points.
loanamount.label: Loan Amount ($)

loanterm.helper: Enter the loan term in years. Common terms are 15 or 30 years.
loanterm.label: Loan Term (years)

points.helper: Enter the number of mortgage points you are considering buying. Each point costs 1% of the loan amount and typically reduces the interest rate by 0.25%.
points.label: Points Purchased

closing_costs.helper: Total fees to refinance (e.g., appraisal, origination). This is not financial advice; consult a professional.
closing_costs.label: Closing Costs

current_balance.helper: The remaining principal amount on your existing mortgage. This is not financial advice; consult a professional.
current_balance.label: Current Loan Balance

current_rate.helper: Your existing mortgage's annual interest rate as a percentage. This is not financial advice; consult a professional.
current_rate.label: Current Interest Rate

new_rate.helper: The proposed annual interest rate for refinancing. This is not financial advice; consult a professional.
new_rate.label: New Interest Rate

remaining_term.helper: Number of months left on your current mortgage. This is not financial advice; consult a professional.
remaining_term.label: Remaining Loan Term (months)

fuel_type.helper: Determines carbon intensity and energy content. Required for environmental loss calculations per ISO 14067.

mpg_value.label: Fuel Economy (MPG)

annual_production_volume.helper: Total number of units produced per year for the measured characteristic.
annual_production_volume.label: Annual Production Volume

cost_per_defect.helper: Average cost incurred when a non-conforming unit is detected internally (scrap, rework, inspection).
cost_per_defect.label: Cost per Defect (Internal)

cost_per_escaped_defect.helper: Average cost when a defect reaches the customer (warranty, liability, lost sales).
cost_per_escaped_defect.label: Cost per Escaped Defect (External)

defect_rate.helper: Parts per million non-conforming based on current measurement system.
defect_rate.label: Current Defect Rate (ppm)

include_hidden_losses.helper: If true, calculates costs from misclassifications due to measurement error.
include_hidden_losses.label: Include Hidden Losses (False Positives/Negatives)

num_appraisers.helper: Number of operators or appraisers performing measurements, minimum 2 for reproducibility estimation.
num_appraisers.label: Number of Appraisers

num_parts.helper: Number of distinct parts selected for the Gage R&R study, typically 5-20 per AIAG MSA guidelines.
num_parts.label: Number of Parts in Study
num_parts.placeholder: Enter number of parts in study

num_trials.helper: Number of repeated measurements per part per appraiser, typically 2-3.
num_trials.label: Number of Trials per Part
num_trials.placeholder: Enter number of trials per part

repeatability_variation.helper: Variation due to equipment (same operator, same part, repeated measurements).
repeatability_variation.label: Repeatability Variation (Std Dev)

reproducibility_variation.helper: Variation due to different operators measuring the same part.
reproducibility_variation.label: Reproducibility Variation (Std Dev)

total_variation.helper: Standard deviation of all measurements in the study, representing total process variation.
total_variation.label: Total Observed Variation (Std Dev)

cost_per_downtime_hour.helper: Lost profit or cost incurred per hour of equipment downtime (lost production, labor, penalties).
cost_per_downtime_hour.label: Cost per Downtime Hour

cost_per_failure_event.helper: Fixed costs per failure: spare parts, logistics, contractor fees, administrative overhead.
cost_per_failure_event.label: Cost per Failure Event (excluding downtime)
cost_per_failure_event.placeholder: Enter cost per failure event (excluding downtime)

industry_benchmark_mtbf.helper: Reference MTBF from industry standards (e.g., WERC, ISO) for comparison.

maintenance_labor_rate.helper: Hourly cost of maintenance personnel (including overhead).
maintenance_labor_rate.label: Maintenance Labor Rate

mtbf.helper: Average time between failures for a repairable system. Higher is better. ISO 14224.
mtbf.label: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)

mttr.helper: Average time to restore a failed system to operational state. Lower is better. Includes diagnosis, repair, testing.
mttr.label: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR)

number_of_units.helper: Total number of identical or similar assets in the fleet.
number_of_units.label: Number of Units

operating_hours_per_year.helper: Total planned operating hours of the equipment per year. Used to calculate failure frequency and downtime.
operating_hours_per_year.label: Operating Hours per Year

reliability_improvement_investment.helper: Annual budget allocated to reliability improvement projects (training, redesign, predictive maintenance).
reliability_improvement_investment.label: Reliability Improvement Investment (annual)

defect_rate.label: Defect Rate

excess_motion_cost_per_unit.label: Excess Motion Cost per Unit
excess_motion_cost_per_unit.placeholder: Enter excess motion cost per unit

include_hidden_losses.helper: If true, includes intangible losses such as lost opportunity cost and brand damage.
include_hidden_losses.label: Include Hidden Losses

industry_type.helper: Industry sector for benchmarking and threshold adjustment.
industry_type.label: Industry Type

inventory_holding_cost_per_unit.helper: Cost of holding one unit in inventory per year (WERC DC Metrics).
inventory_holding_cost_per_unit.label: Inventory Holding Cost per Unit
inventory_holding_cost_per_unit.placeholder: Enter inventory holding cost per unit

labor_cost_per_hour.helper: Average fully burdened labor cost per hour for direct operators.
labor_cost_per_hour.label: Labor Cost per Hour

material_cost_per_unit.helper: Direct material cost per unit of product, including raw materials and purchased components.
material_cost_per_unit.label: Material Cost per Unit

overhead_rate.helper: Percentage of direct labor cost allocated to manufacturing overhead (ISO 9001 cost accounting).
overhead_rate.label: Overhead Rate

overproduction_quantity.helper: Number of units produced beyond customer demand (Muda of overproduction).
overproduction_quantity.label: Overproduction Quantity

processing_waste_per_unit.helper: Cost of unnecessary processing steps or non-value-added operations.
processing_waste_per_unit.label: Processing Waste per Unit

production_volume.helper: Total number of units produced in the analysis period.
production_volume.label: Production Volume

rework_time_per_unit.helper: Average time spent reworking a defective unit.
rework_time_per_unit.label: Rework Time per Unit

scrap_rate.helper: Percentage of defective units that are scrapped (cannot be reworked).
scrap_rate.label: Scrap Rate

shift_hours_per_day.helper: Number of operating hours per shift.
shift_hours_per_day.label: Shift Hours per Day

transportation_cost_per_unit.helper: Cost of moving materials or products between processes (Lean waste of transportation).
transportation_cost_per_unit.label: Transportation Cost per Unit

waiting_time_per_unit.helper: Average idle time per unit due to bottlenecks, material shortages, or equipment downtime (Lean Takt time analysis).
waiting_time_per_unit.label: Waiting Time per Unit

working_days_per_year.helper: Number of working days per year.
working_days_per_year.label: Working Days per Year

asset_turnover.helper: Asset turnover measures efficiency of asset use in generating sales; higher is better.
asset_turnover.label: Asset Turnover

current_assets.helper: Current assets are short-term resources; used to assess liquidity and working capital.
current_assets.label: Current Assets

current_liabilities.helper: Current liabilities are short-term obligations; used to compute current ratio.
current_liabilities.label: Current Liabilities

gross_margin.helper: Gross margin measures profitability from sales after cost of goods sold; higher is better.
gross_margin.label: Gross Margin

long_term_debt.helper: Long-term debt indicates leverage; decreasing debt improves financial strength.
long_term_debt.label: Long-Term Debt

net_income.helper: Positive net income indicates profitability, a fundamental sign of financial health.
net_income.label: Net Income

operating_cash_flow.helper: Operating cash flow measures cash generated from core operations, a key liquidity indicator.
operating_cash_flow.label: Operating Cash Flow

total_assets.helper: Total assets represent the company's resources; used to compute return on assets.
total_assets.label: Total Assets

account_currency.helper: Select the currency your trading account is denominated in. This is used to convert the pip value to your account currency.
account_currency.label: Account Currency

currency_pair.helper: Select the currency pair you are trading. This determines the pip value calculation based on the quote currency.
currency_pair.label: Currency Pair

exchange_rate.helper: Current exchange rate of the currency pair. For pairs where USD is the quote currency, this is the rate to convert pip value to USD.
exchange_rate.label: Exchange Rate

trade_size.helper: Enter the number of units of the base currency you are trading. Standard lot is 100,000 units.
trade_size.label: Trade Size (units)

elevation_change.helper: Positive if outlet is higher than inlet. Affects gravitational head.
elevation_change.label: Elevation Change

flow_rate.helper: Design flow rate. Must be within pump capacity and pipe rating.
flow_rate.label: Volumetric Flow Rate

flow_regime.helper: Select 'auto' for Reynolds-based determination. Override for sensitivity analysis.
flow_regime.label: Flow Regime Assumption

fluid_density.helper: Density at operating temperature. Water = 1000, oil ≈ 850.
fluid_density.label: Fluid Density

fluid_viscosity.helper: Dynamic viscosity at operating temperature. Water = 0.001, heavy oil up to 1.
fluid_viscosity.label: Dynamic Viscosity

include_measurement_uncertainty.helper: When true, applies GUM (Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement) propagation to final result.
include_measurement_uncertainty.label: Include Measurement Uncertainty

minor_loss_coefficient.helper: Sum of K factors for fittings, valves, bends. Per Crane Technical Paper No. 410.
minor_loss_coefficient.label: Minor Loss Coefficient (K)

pipe_diameter.helper: Nominal pipe diameter per ISO 6708. Affects cross-sectional area and velocity.
pipe_diameter.label: Pipe Inner Diameter

pipe_length.helper: Total straight pipe length. Used for friction loss calculation.
pipe_length.label: Pipe Length

roughness.helper: Absolute roughness height. Steel = 0.000045, concrete = 0.001. Per ISO 9906.
roughness.label: Pipe Roughness

geopolitical_risk_index.helper: Composite index of political instability, trade restrictions, and regional conflicts affecting supply chain.

inventory_buffer_days.helper: Average days of inventory held to absorb demand or supply shocks. Based on lean inventory principles.

lead_time_variability.helper: Standard deviation of lead time divided by mean lead time. Higher values indicate unpredictable supply.

quality_defect_rate.helper: Parts per million defective from incoming inspection. Six Sigma target is < 3.4 ppm.

single_source_dependency.helper: Percentage of critical components sourced from a single supplier. High dependency increases risk.

supplier_reliability_score.helper: Historical on-time delivery and quality performance of key suppliers. Higher score indicates better reliability.
supplier_reliability_score.label: Supplier Reliability Score (0-100)

transportation_disruption_probability.helper: Estimated likelihood of a major transportation disruption (port closure, strike, etc.) in the next quarter.
transportation_disruption_probability.label: Transportation Disruption Probability (0-1)

use_advanced_hedging.helper: If true, applies financial and operational hedging models to mitigate currency and commodity risks.

cost_at_limit.helper: Estimated financial loss (rework, scrap, warranty) when a unit is exactly at the specification limit.
cost_at_limit.label: Cost at Specification Limit (A₀)

deviation_at_limit.helper: Distance from target to the nearest specification limit (Δ = min(T-LSL, USL-T)).
deviation_at_limit.label: Deviation at Limit (Δ)

include_hidden_factory.helper: If true, adds estimated losses from rework, inspection, and scrap that are not captured by the basic loss function.
include_hidden_factory.label: Include Hidden Factory Losses

inspection_rate.helper: Percentage of units inspected. Used to adjust hidden loss estimates.
inspection_rate.label: Inspection Rate (%)

loss_function_type.helper: Type of quality characteristic: nominal-is-best (target), smaller-is-better (e.g., impurities), larger-is-better (e.g., strength).
loss_function_type.label: Loss Function Type

lower_spec_limit.helper: Minimum acceptable value per engineering or customer requirement.
lower_spec_limit.label: Lower Specification Limit (LSL)

process_mean.helper: Current average of the process output.
process_mean.label: Process Mean (μ)

production_volume.helper: Total number of units produced in the period.
production_volume.label: Production Volume (N)

target_value.helper: The ideal nominal specification for the product characteristic (e.g., length, weight, voltage).
target_value.label: Target Value (T)

upper_spec_limit.helper: Maximum acceptable value per engineering or customer requirement.
upper_spec_limit.label: Upper Specification Limit (USL)

gross_annual_salary.helper: Total annual earnings before any deductions, used as the base for tax and social security calculations.
gross_annual_salary.label: Gross Annual Salary

other_deductions.helper: Additional annual deductions such as health insurance premiums, retirement contributions, or garnishments.
other_deductions.label: Other Deductions

social_security_rate.helper: Percentage of gross salary contributed to social security, including Medicare and Social Security in the US.
social_security_rate.label: Social Security Rate

tax_rate.helper: Effective income tax rate as a percentage, applied to gross salary to compute income tax deduction.
tax_rate.label: Tax Rate

available_work_time_seconds.helper: Total planned production time per shift excluding breaks and planned downtime.
available_work_time_seconds.label: Available Work Time per Shift (seconds)
available_work_time_seconds.placeholder: Enter available work time per shift (seconds)

batch_size.helper: Number of units produced consecutively before a changeover.
batch_size.label: Average Batch Size (units)

changeover_time_minutes.helper: Time required to switch from one product variant to another, including setup and adjustments.
changeover_time_minutes.label: Average Changeover Time (minutes)

customer_demand_per_shift.helper: Number of units required by the customer per shift.
customer_demand_per_shift.label: Customer Demand per Shift (units)

cycle_time_seconds.helper: Measured average time to complete one unit of product at the bottleneck operation.
cycle_time_seconds.label: Actual Cycle Time (seconds)

demand_variability_coefficient.helper: Coefficient of variation of daily customer demand; used to quantify demand uncertainty.
demand_variability_coefficient.label: Demand Variability Coefficient (CV)

flexibility_strategy.helper: Production strategy to handle demand variability: mixed-model (quick changeovers), dedicated lines (fixed product lines), chase demand (vary capacity), or level schedule (stable production with inventory).
flexibility_strategy.label: Flexibility Strategy

labor_cost_per_hour.helper: Fully loaded hourly wage including benefits for direct production operators.
labor_cost_per_hour.label: Direct Labor Cost per Hour ($)
labor_cost_per_hour.placeholder: Enter direct labor cost per hour ($)

overhead_rate_percent.helper: Percentage of direct labor cost allocated to manufacturing overhead (e.g., supervision, utilities, maintenance).
overhead_rate_percent.label: Overhead Rate (%)

applied_load.helper: Service load acting on the weld joint.
applied_load.label: Applied Load

base_material_yield_strength.helper: Yield strength of the weaker base material (ISO 898 / ASTM A36).
base_material_yield_strength.label: Base Material Yield Strength

inspection_method.helper: NDT method used; affects confidence factor.
inspection_method.label: Inspection Method

is_fatigue_loading.helper: If true, applies S-N curve reduction per AWS D1.1 fatigue categories.
is_fatigue_loading.label: Fatigue Loading

load_angle.helper: Angle between applied load and weld longitudinal axis. 90° = transverse, 0° = longitudinal.
load_angle.label: Load Angle (from weld axis)

quality_level.helper: ISO 5817 weld quality level: B=stringent, C=moderate, D=basic.
quality_level.label: Quality Level (ISO 5817)

throat_thickness.helper: Minimum throat dimension per ISO 5817 quality level B.
throat_thickness.label: Effective Throat Thickness

weld_length.helper: Total length of the weld seam.
weld_length.label: Weld Length

weld_material_tensile_strength.helper: Tensile strength of filler metal (e.g., ER70S-6).
weld_material_tensile_strength.label: Weld Material Tensile Strength

weld_type.helper: Determines the geometry factor and stress distribution model per AWS D1.1.
weld_type.label: Weld Type

consumable_cost_per_kg.helper: Cost of filler metal per kilogram.
consumable_cost_per_kg.label: Consumable Cost per kg

deposition_efficiency.helper: Ratio of deposited metal to consumed filler. Varies by process: SMAW~65%, GMAW~90%, FCAW~85%, SAW~99%, GTAW~95%.
deposition_efficiency.label: Deposition Efficiency

groove_angle.helper: Included angle of V-groove. Standard range per AWS D1.1.
groove_angle.label: Groove Angle (α)

joint_type.helper: Select the weld joint geometry per ISO 9692. Affects volume calculation formula.
joint_type.label: Joint Type

labor_rate.helper: Hourly cost of welder including overhead.
labor_rate.label: Labor Rate

leg_length.helper: Fillet weld leg size. Only used when joint_type = fillet.
leg_length.label: Leg Length (for fillet)

material_density.helper: Density of weld metal. Steel ~7.85, Aluminum ~2.7, Stainless ~8.0.
material_density.label: Material Density

operator_factor.helper: Percentage of total time that the welder is actually depositing metal. Typical range 20-50%.
operator_factor.label: Operator Factor

overhead_rate.helper: Percentage added to direct labor for indirect costs.
overhead_rate.label: Overhead Rate

plate_thickness.helper: Thickness of the base metal. Critical for groove volume calculation.
plate_thickness.label: Plate Thickness (t)

root_face.helper: Land at the root of the groove. Affects effective depth.
root_face.label: Root Face (f)

root_gap.helper: Gap between plates at root. Increases weld volume.
root_gap.label: Root Gap (g)

use_premium_data.helper: If true, adjusts deposition efficiency and operator factor based on historical data from premium database.
use_premium_data.label: Use Premium Data (historical trends)

weld_length.helper: Total length of the weld seam.
weld_length.label: Weld Length (L)

weld_process.helper: Select the welding process. Affects deposition efficiency and labor rate.
weld_process.label: Welding Process

weld_speed.helper: Travel speed of the welding arc. Affects deposition rate and time.
weld_speed.label: Weld Speed

wire_diameter.helper: Diameter of filler wire. Affects cross-sectional area and deposition rate.
wire_diameter.label: Wire Diameter

wire_feed_speed.helper: Only for GMAW/FCAW processes. Used to calculate deposition rate.
wire_feed_speed.label: Wire Feed Speed (if applicable)

primary_value.helper: Primary input for Welding Calculator.
primary_value.label: Primary value

secondary_value.helper: Secondary input for Welding Calculator.
secondary_value.label: Secondary value

tertiary_value.helper: Optional modifier for Welding Calculator.
tertiary_value.label: Tertiary value

deposition_efficiency.helper: Ratio of deposited weld metal to total filler metal consumed (includes spatter and losses).
deposition_efficiency.label: Deposition Efficiency (%)

filler_wire_cost_per_kg.helper: Cost per kilogram of filler wire or electrode.
filler_wire_cost_per_kg.label: Filler Wire Cost (USD/kg)

gas_cost_per_liter.helper: Cost per liter of shielding gas (e.g., Argon/CO2 mix).
gas_cost_per_liter.label: Shielding Gas Cost (USD/L)

gas_flow_rate_l_per_min.helper: Flow rate of shielding gas during welding.
gas_flow_rate_l_per_min.label: Gas Flow Rate (L/min)

labor_rate_per_hour.helper: Fully burdened hourly labor cost for welder.
labor_rate_per_hour.label: Labor Rate (USD/hour)

material_density_g_per_cm3.helper: Density of the base and filler material (typical steel ~7.85).
material_density_g_per_cm3.label: Material Density (g/cm³)

overhead_rate_per_hour.helper: Facility and equipment overhead allocated per hour of welding.
overhead_rate_per_hour.label: Overhead Rate (USD/hour)

rework_cost_factor.helper: Multiplier on original cost to account for rework (cutting, re-welding, inspection).
rework_cost_factor.label: Rework Cost Factor

rework_rate_percent.helper: Percentage of welds requiring rework (defect rate).
rework_rate_percent.label: Rework Rate (%)

use_premium_material.helper: Toggle to apply a 20% cost premium for high-performance filler wire.
use_premium_material.label: Use Premium Filler Material

weld_length_mm.helper: Total length of the weld joint to be deposited.
weld_length_mm.label: Weld Length (mm)

weld_process_type.helper: Standard arc welding process per AWS A3.0.
weld_process_type.label: Welding Process Type

weld_throat_mm.helper: Effective throat thickness of the fillet or groove weld.
weld_throat_mm.label: Weld Throat Thickness (mm)

welding_power_kw.helper: Average power consumption of the welding machine during operation.
welding_power_kw.label: Welding Power (kW)

welding_speed_mm_per_min.helper: Travel speed of the welding torch along the joint.
welding_speed_mm_per_min.label: Welding Speed (mm/min)

breakdownChart.actionProHint: Unlock full action playbooks with Pro.

actions.annualloss: Prioritize the highest annual-loss drivers in your kaizen backlog.
actions.dailyloss: Validate daily loss assumptions with shop-floor data before scaling actions.
actions.dataconfidencemultiplier: Improve data confidence with meter readings, time studies, or ERP extracts.
actions.default: Review root causes with your operations team and define a measurable improvement target.
actions.defects: Stand up a quality improvement team, run root-cause analysis, and add poka-yoke where practical.
actions.inventory: Review stock levels, apply JIT where possible, and run ABC analysis on slow movers.
actions.motion: Apply 5S and ergonomics improvements to reduce non-value movement.
actions.overprocessing: Value-stream the process and remove steps the customer does not pay for.
actions.overproduction: Align production planning to demand, review Kanban levels, and reduce batch sizes where feasible.
actions.transport: Redesign material flow, shorten routes, and consolidate transfer batches.
actions.waiting: Analyze bottlenecks, apply SMED to cut setup time, and improve preventive maintenance.

breakdownChart.clickHint: Click a bar or slice to view related inputs. Use the tooltip for action suggestions.

descriptions.annualloss: Annualized loss projection based on frequency and working days.
descriptions.dailyloss: Estimated daily monetary loss from the current waste profile.
descriptions.dataconfidencemultiplier: Adjustment factor reflecting input data confidence.
descriptions.default: Component of the calculated loss breakdown.
descriptions.defects: Scrap and rework. Weak quality control, process variation, or material issues.
descriptions.inventory: Excess stock. Driven by forecast error, high safety stock, or slow-moving items.
descriptions.motion: Unnecessary operator movement. Missing 5S, ergonomics issues, or hard-to-reach tools/materials.
descriptions.overprocessing: Extra processing steps. Unneeded inspections, approvals, or over-engineering.
descriptions.overproduction: Excess production beyond demand. Often caused by poor forecasting, large batch habits, or unbalanced production plans.
descriptions.transport: Unnecessary movement. Poor layout, repeated handling, or unoptimized routes.
descriptions.waiting: Idle time losses. Caused by bottlenecks, unplanned downtime, or material shortages.

homeAbout.beliefSubtitle: Precision in calculation is the foundation of reliable performance.
homeAbout.highlightPrimary: A platform to expose hidden losses,
homeAbout.highlightSecondary: and transform computation into reliable decision support.
homeAbout.paragraph1: Profit often leaks from businesses through unnoticed costs, not sales. Incorrect pricing, calculation errors, waste, efficiency loss, and weak quoting discipline silently erode margins over time.
homeAbout.paragraph2: SectorCalc is engineered to make these invisible losses quantifiable. It elevates cost, proposal, efficiency, and risk decisions onto a faster, more transparent, and reliable baseline.
homeAbout.title: About Us

faq.a: You can preview the approach for free; full verdict, metrics, and premium decision summary are included in SectorCalc Pro.
No. It is a technical simulation and decision-support tool, not financial, legal, or engineering advice. Verify results before business decisions.
Results reflect the inputs you provide and SectorCalc's deterministic engine. Better inputs produce a sharper, more defensible read.
Yes. Premium results can be exported as decision summaries with a Calculation Summary of how the numbers were derived.
It is tuned for its sector's pricing and margin patterns, and it sits alongside the rest of SectorCalc's 27-sector toolset.
Open the analyzer, enter %1$s, and review %1$s. Adjust inputs to stress-test the scenario before you commit to a price.

premiumSeo.h1: %1$s: price the job and protect the margin
premiumSeo.help: It applies SectorCalc's deterministic calculation engine to your inputs, then highlights the factors that move the result the most. You get a structured read you can defend, not a black-box number.
premiumSeo.howTo: Enter %1$s for your %1$s scenario. SectorCalc runs governed, deterministic formulas and returns %1$s. Add optional fields when you have them to sharpen threshold and risk reads.
premiumSeo.inputs: Core fields include %1$s (%1$s inputs in total). Required fields stay minimal; optional ones refine the verdict when your data is ready.
premiumSeo.intro: The %1$s helps you pressure-test a job before you commit to a price. It turns your costs, quantities, and rates into a clear margin and risk read, so you can spot the hidden drivers that quietly erode profit and decide with confidence instead of guessing on the bid.
premiumSeo.metaDescription: How the %1$s works, who it is for, what to enter, and how to read the result. Pricing and margin-risk decision support from SectorCalc.
premiumSeo.method: The %1$s uses SectorCalc's formula governance layer: validated inputs, a deterministic calculation pipeline, and threshold checks. It is a technical simulation — not financial, legal, or engineering advice.
premiumSeo.mistakes: Stale labor or material rates, skipped optional inputs that capture hidden loss, mixed units, and treating the read as guaranteed profit are frequent errors. %1$s Verify inputs before business decisions.
premiumSeo.outputs: %1$s returns %1$s with metrics, threshold alerts, and risk drivers — so you see why the number lands where it does and what to adjust.
premiumSeo.problem: Most jobs lose margin long before the invoice, when setup, rework, overruns, and underpriced line items are never added up before the quote goes out. The %1$s makes those drivers visible so they stop surprising you.
premiumSeo.trust: Premium results can include a Calculation Summary of inputs, assumptions, and validation — saved as exportable decision summaries for internal review.
premiumSeo.who: Operators, estimators, owners, and managers who price work, review bids, or need a consistent baseline across a team will get the most from the %1$s.

privacy.heroDescription: What data we collect, how we use it, and your rights.
privacy.intro: Your privacy is important to us. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights.

meta.description: How SectorCalc collects, uses, and protects your personal data.
meta.title: Privacy Policy

section1.content: We collect personal information you provide (e.g., email address when you register). We also automatically collect usage data (IP address, browser type, pages visited) to improve our tools.
section1.title: 1. Information We Collect

section2.content: We use your data to operate, maintain, and improve SectorCalc, to process payments, and to communicate with you about your account or updates.
section2.title: 2. How We Use Your Information

section3.content: We use essential cookies for authentication and optional analytics cookies (e.g., Google Analytics) to analyze usage. You can disable non-essential cookies in your browser settings.
section3.title: 3. Cookies & Tracking

section4.content: We do not sell your personal data. We share data only with trusted third-party services (payment processors — Stripe/Paddle, hosting — Vercel) as necessary to provide our services.
section4.title: 4. Data Sharing

section5.content: We retain your data as long as your account is active. We implement reasonable security measures, but no method of transmission over the Internet is 100% secure.
section5.title: 5. Data Retention & Security

section6.content: You may request access, correction, or deletion of your personal data by contacting us at info@sectorcalc.com.
section6.title: 6. Your Rights

section7.content: Our services are not directed to individuals under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.
section7.title: 7. Children's Privacy

section8.content: We may update this policy from time to time. The "Effective" date at the top indicates when the latest version was posted.
section8.title: 8. Changes to This Policy

section9.content: The data controller for SectorCalc is:
section9.title: 9. Data Controller

privacy.title: Privacy Policy

refundPolicy.heroDescription: Refund terms for credit purchases and subscriptions.
refundPolicy.intro: We want you to be satisfied with your purchase. Please read this Refund Policy carefully.

meta.description: Refund terms for credit purchases and subscriptions on SectorCalc.
meta.title: Refund Policy

section1.content: Credits: Purchases of credits are final and non-refundable. Unused credits expire 12 months after purchase. No cash refunds will be issued for unused credits. Subscriptions: If you purchase a recurring subscription, you may request a full refund within 14 days of your first payment, provided you have not used any premium tool during that period. After 14 days or after first use, subscription fees are non-refundable.
section1.title: 1. Credits & Subscriptions

section2.content: If a premium tool produces demonstrably incorrect results due to a bug in our system, we will refund the credits used for that calculation (up to the number of credits spent) or provide replacement credits. Please report the issue with screenshots and details to info@sectorcalc.com.
section2.title: 2. Faulty or Incorrect Tools

section3.content: To request a refund, contact us at info@sectorcalc.com with your transaction ID (from your payment provider) and the reason for the request. We will respond within 7–14 business days.
section3.title: 3. How to Request a Refund

section4.content: If you initiate a chargeback without first contacting us, we may suspend your account until the matter is resolved.
section4.title: 4. Chargebacks

section5.content: We reserve the right to modify this refund policy at any time. Changes will be posted on this page.
section5.title: 5. Changes to this Policy

section6.content: For refund requests or billing questions, contact us using the details below.
section6.title: 6. Contact Information
